scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (12 rules + cheat-asm stripped); frame-floor residual

sessions.lever: identified forbidden dead-frame floor (target 72B vs GCC 40B); 12 rules patch frame+offsets

reviewer.evidence: Forbidden dead-frame floor: matching the 72-byte target frame (GCC produces 40) requires s32 pad[8] (forbidden dead local array) or register pins + frame-size regfix (cheats). No semantically-recoverable local accounts for the 32 dead bytes. Per dead-vars-local-array this is park/canonical-asm, not COMPLETED-C.

remaining_gap.summary: Target frame 72 bytes vs GCC 40; the 12 rules patch the frame size and all 8 register save/restore offsets + a prologue reorder + an INLINE_MOVE_ALIASING. The ~32 extra frame bytes have no recoverable semantic local (register-only body + one stacked 5th call arg). HEAD also carries a register asm($16) pin + __asm__(move %0,%1).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: frame-size subst (40->72) + 8 save/restore offset substs + prologue reorder + addu $16 INLINE_MOVE_ALIASING — i.e. the dead-frame cheat, forbidden by dead-vars-local-array.

next_hypotheses: Canonical-asm authorization (genuinely hand-written prologue / unrecoverable frame).

If a future agent can identify what the 32 frame bytes semantically held in the ORIGINAL (a named buffer used by the call-loop), reconstruct it with a meaningful name — but the asm shows no stack-buffer use, so this is unlikely.
Sibling AddTbpOfst_80047EE8 was parked for the identical floor — resolve together.

rejected_forms.form: s32 pad[8] / register pins + frame-size regfix
rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN dead-frame coercion per dead-vars-local-array; engine refuses completion.
